subject: Social Science (Economics)
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyse the process of globalisation
Evaluate the role and strategies of MNCs
Interpret FDI and trade data trends
Examine the impact of liberalisation (1991 reforms)
Assess the role of WTO in global trade governance
Develop case-based and data interpretation skills

lesson_plan.title: Globalisation: Concept and Drivers
teaching_strategy: Case example (Global supply chain of a smartphone)
student_activities: Trace components of a product from different countries
Prepare flowchart of global production network

lesson_plan.title: Multinational Corporations (MNCs) – Strategies and Impact
student_activities: Explain advantages and risks of FDI

lesson_plan.title: Foreign Trade and Trade Barriers – Analytical View
Liberalisation Reforms (1991) – Impact Analysis
teaching_strategy: Comparative analysis (Indian companies before and after reforms)
student_activities: Write analytical paragraph on impact on small producers

lesson_plan.title: Role of WTO and Global Trade Governance
student_activities: Debate: Is WTO fair to developing countries?
List pros and cons of WTO rules

lesson_plan.title: Impact of Globalisation in India – Sectoral Analysis
topics_covered: Impact on workers and small producers
Growth of IT and service sector
teaching_strategy: Case study (Small textile unit vs MNC)
student_activities: Suggest measures to protect small producers

lesson_plan.title: Integrated Data Interpretation and Assessment
student_activities: Write structured answer: 'Is globalisation beneficial for India?'

teacher_tip: Encourage students to use real examples and data while answering. Focus on building analytical reasoning rather than memorising definitions.
